_comment: Copy to master.config.json (e.g. deploy/config/). ONE file to edit per deployment. Use {runtimeRoot} in paths. Secrets only in credentials.json next to this file (see credentials.example.json).

kafka._comment_clientInstallDir: Relative folder under runtimeRoot with bin/kafka-topics.sh, OR absolute path to the Kafka install dir (no /bin). If missing under runtime (common: bind-mount replaces /opt/kafka-usermgmt), the app falls back to image CLI at /opt/apache-kafka/kafka_2.13-3.6.1/bin.
kafka._comment_k8sSecretName: Confluent on OCP: default secret holding plain SASL user list (plain-users.json). Other secrets (kafka-admin-client-credentials, kafka-client-side-credentials, *-apikeys) are different; override only if your install renames this one.

oc._comment: The portal never creates OpenShift contexts. Mount a kubeconfig that already lists every context you reference in sites/environments (oc config get-contexts NAME). Per-namespace contexts on one cluster are normal.
oc._comment_kubeconfig: Portal never creates kubeconfig files. Single cluster: oc login -> config. Multi-cluster merged file: optional path e.g. .../config-both. If both files exist, app prefers more contexts; on a tie, prefers config.
oc._comment_loginServers: Keys must match each site ocContext. Use the same cluster: server URL as in kubeconfig (clusters[].cluster.server) for every context on that cluster.

_comment_fallbackSites: Prod-style two clusters. For dev/sit/uat on one cluster use environments.*.sites ocContext cwdc-dev | cwdc-sit | cwdc-uat (names must match oc config get-contexts).
_comment_environments_sites: Each environment may have sites: [ { name, ocContext, namespace, apiServer? }, ... ]. One row per kube context+namespace (multi-region = multiple objects). Optional apiServer is copied into oc.loginServers[ocContext] on save (repeat same URL for one cluster).
_comment_environments_kafka: With environments.enabled true: each env id (e.g. dev, sit, uat) uses {runtimeRoot}/configs/kafka-client-master-{id}.properties and kafka-client-{id}.properties — no unsuffixed default while that env is selected. Optional bootstrapServers; optional adminPropertiesFile / clientPropertiesFile to override filenames; adminConfig / clientConfig = path under runtimeRoot or absolute.

environments.label: System Integration Test
environments._comment_bootstrapServers: Optional: if SIT brokers use a different route than kafka.bootstrapServers, set host:port here — portal + GEN_ACTIVE_ENV_ID use it.
